title: Ocsin

The '''Ocsin''', also known as the '''Ocsinin''', were a [[near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]] with [[Color/Legends|white]] skin and black, pupilless [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] that [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from the [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] celestial body [[Farana/Legends|Farana]]. Believed to be descended from the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] culture of the [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]], the Ocsin established first contact with the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] at some point before [[4500 BBY]]. The species' members included [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] and [[Scout/Legends|explorers]], and it established at least several dozen [[Colonist/Legends|colonies]] in Farana's [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighborhood, including the [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] [[Ocsin (planet)|Ocsin]] and [[Saclas]]. The [[Gender/Legends|female]] Ocsin [[Cene Gilvent]] grew up on Saclas, eventually traveling offworld where she became one of the founding members of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] group of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] afficionados.

==Biology and appearance==
The Ocsin,<ref name="TEA" /> also referred to as the Ocsinin,<ref name="AIR" /> were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[near-Human]] [[Species/Legends|species]].<ref name="TEA" /> Most Ocsin had glaringly [[Color/Legends|white]] skin and black, pupilless [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. One member of the species, [[Cene Gilvent]], had long yellow [[Hair/Legends|hair]] and a body build that was large for Ocsin but relatively slender for [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] in general.<ref name="AIR" />

==Society and culture==
The Ocsin identified as belonging to at least one—female—[[Gender/Legends|gender]].<ref name="AIR" /> Their society was organized into clans,<ref name="TEA" /> and the species also made use of its own [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]].<ref name="AIR" />

==History==
The Ocsin [[Homeworld/Legends|hailed]] from the foggy [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]] celestial body [[Farana/Legends|Farana]]. They were believed to have originated with the [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Tionese (group)/Legends|Tionese]] culture, which in turn had come into being from [[Sleeper ship/Legends|sleeper ship]] missions that [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Tion Cluster/Legends|Tion Cluster]] around [[27,000 BBY/Legends|27,000 BBY]]. At some point before [[4500 BBY]], [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] from the Human-dominated [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] reached Farana's [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] neighborhood and made first contact with the Ocsin.<ref name="TEA" /> Farana was never a part of the Republic or its [[Declaration of a New Order|successor]], the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="EotECR">[[Star Wars: Edge of the Empire Core Rulebook|''Star Wars: Edge of the Empire'' Core Rulebook]]</ref>

==Ocsin in the galaxy==
Members of the Ocsin species included cunning [[Merchant/Legends|merchants]] and bold explorers.<ref name="TEA" /> Over [[Time/Legends|time]], Ocsin scouts compiled a wealth of [[Astrogation/Legends|navigational]] data about Wild Space.<ref name="EotECR" /> The species established several dozen colonies Rimward of [[Farana (region)/Legends|Farana]], the region of [[Realspace/Legends|space]] in which their homeworld was situated. They also settled the stormy, gloomy [[Planet/Legends|world]] known as [[Ocsin (planet)|Ocsin]] and the barren<ref name="TEA" /> planet<ref name="AIR" /> [[Saclas]],<ref name="TEA" /> both in the Outer Rim.<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Ocsin system/Legends|Ocsin]] and [[Saclas system/Legends|Saclas systems]]</ref> The minor [[Agriworld/Legends|farming]]<ref name="TEA" /> planet<ref name="MT">{{WEGCite|story=Money Troubles|book=Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game, Second Edition|text=''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'', Second Edition}}</ref> [[Fether]], situated near Ocsin, hosted a sizeable Ocsin population as well.<ref name="TEA" />

[[File:Corporate_sector.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Ocsin hailed from Farana, which was situated to the northeast of what would eventually become the Corporate Sector.]]
A few [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], the [[Corporate Sector Authority/Legends|Corporate Sector Authority]], the Galactic Empire–affiliated entity administrating Farana's neighboring [[Corporate Sector/Legends|Corporate Sector]], annexed Ocsin, and Saclas eventually also fell within the territory of the [[Sector/Legends|sector]], near its border with the [[Wyl sector/Legends|Wyl sector]]. Around that time, Ocsin traders could be encountered at [[Issagra space station|Issagra Station]] in the Corporate Sector's [[Issagra system/Legends|Issagra system]] and also on Saclas, which they visited while traveling down the [[Solenbaran Merchant Route]] [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] to reach [[Imperial Space|Imperial-controlled space]]. In addition, Ocsin [[Spacer/Legends|spacers]] warned the curious not to travel to the [[Mytus system/Legends|Mytus system]] located beyond Farana due to the Corporate Sector Authority maintaining a [[Stars' End/Legends|secret prison facility]] there.<ref name="TEA" />

The female Ocsin Cene Gilvent grew up on Saclas, where, during her late teenage years, she earned enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] harvesting [[gelatinous pet slime]]s to buy passage to the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. Subsequently, she was hired by the [[Zuliria Museum of Antiquities]] on the Mid Rim celestial body [[Zuliria]] and eventually became one of the founding members of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]], a group of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] afficionados who began challenging and attacking various individuals in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] that they believed posed a worthy challenge to their skills. After Gilvent had attacked a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldier]] on the celestial body [[Fislan]]<ref name="AIR" /> of the Outer Rim's [[Mortex sector/Legends|Mortex sector]],<ref name="EAOC Fislan">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Fislan system/Legends|Fislan system]]</ref> at some point by [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> [[Major/Legends|Major]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]] submitted a [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] [[SecuriDex]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] on the Ocsin [[swordmaster]]. The document, however, erroneously listed Gilvent's species as Human.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Ocsin were originally introduced as the Ocsinin in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. In the book, the Ocsin Cene Gilvent was illustrated by [[Matt Busch]].<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Daniel Wallace]] and [[Jason Fry]] subsequently introduced the name "Ocsin" for the species.<ref name="TEA" />
